*** 2 INJ *** A EF-1 TORNADO OCCURRED 7 MI SE OF FRIERSON LA SNAPPING HARDWOOD TREES. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED NORTHEAST INTO RED RIVER PARISH...15 MI WNW OF HALL SUMMIT CAUSING DAMAGE TO A HOME AND TREES ON PARISH RD 410. IT MOVED INTO CADDO PARISH AND CROSSED HWY 1...15 MILES SE OF SHREVEPORT CITY LIMITS CAUSING DAMAGE TO 2 VEHICLES AND 2 TRAILERS AT A NATURAL GAS SITE OFF HWY 1. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED AS IT APPROACHED THE RED RIVER. THE PATH WAS 7 MI LONG AND 300 YDS WIDE.

A SUPERCELL MOVED THROUGH MC CURTAIN COUNTY ON THE AFTERNOON OF MAY 30TH. THE STORM PRODUCED AN EF1 TORNADO ABOUT 6 MILES SOUTHEAST OF WATSON OKLAHOMA. THE TORNADO MAINLY UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES...AS IT MOVED THROUGH NORTHEAST MC CURTAIN COUNTY. A TREE FELL ON ONE HOME IN THIS RURAL COMMUNITY...CAUSING DAMAGE TO THE ROOF. THE TORNADO CROSSED THE STATE LINE INTO POLK COUNTY ARKANSAS ABOUT 3 MILES SOUTHWEST OF COVE ARKANSAS.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 1233 AM IN RED RIVER PARISH...JUST EAST OF THE RED RIVER...AND ABOUT A MILE SOUTH OF THE BOSSIER/RED RIVER PARISH LINE ON HWY 515. THE DAMAGE PATH MAINLY CONSISTED OF BOTH HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. THE TORNADO WAS UP AND DOWN ALONG A 10 MILE PATH...BEING NEARLY A MILE WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT ALONG SPRINGHILL CHURCH ROAD. MAX WIND SPEEDS OF 100 TO 110 MPH WERE ESTIMATED ALONG THE PATH.
